cases.title: The Arctic Circle (66 ½° N)
cases.description: The northernmost major line of latitude. It marks the start of the Polar Zone.
steps: Location: It is located at 66 ½° North.
Countries: It passes through Russia, Canada, Norway, Sweden, Finland, and USA (Alaska).
Phenomenon: North of this line, the sun does not set for at least one day in summer (Midnight Sun) and does not rise for one day in winter.

cases.title: The Antarctic Circle (66 ½° S)
cases.description: The southern counterpart surrounding the continent of Antarctica.
steps: Location: It is located at 66 ½° South.
Continent: It surrounds the frozen continent of Antarctica.
No Inhabitants: Unlike the Arctic, there are no permanent countries here, only research stations.
It is the coldest place on Earth.

cases.title: International Date Line (180°)
cases.description: The line where the calendar date changes.
steps: Location: It runs along the 180° Meridian, opposite the Prime Meridian.
Function: It separates two consecutive calendar days.
Rule: Crossing it from East to West adds a day. Crossing West to East subtracts a day.
It passes through the Pacific Ocean to avoid splitting countries.
